Flex Scanner Skeleton.description: Complete standard Flex scanner: %option directives, a prologue with the parser header, three abbreviations (integer, whitespace, identifier), and rules for each. The third tabstop names the token-factory function used in both the INT and ID return statements.

RE-flex Scanner Skeleton.description: Complete RE-flex scanner: bison-complete/bison-locations options for seamless C++ Bison integration, %top{} for includes, abbreviations, and a placeholder rules section. Tabstops: (1) parser class, (2) namespace, (3) lexer class, (4) parser header, (5) extra code, (6) first rule.

Rule with Action.description: Single Flex rule: a pattern followed by a C action block. The pattern is matched against the input; yytext holds the matched text inside the action.

Rule with Start Condition.description: Flex rule that fires only when the scanner is in a specific start condition. Use <CONDITION>pattern to restrict a rule to one state, or <*>pattern to match in all states. Declare the condition with %x (exclusive) or %s (inclusive).

Start Condition Exclusive.description: Declares an exclusive start condition. While in an exclusive state, only rules explicitly tagged with <SC_NAME> are active — untagged rules are suppressed. Use for string literals, block comments, and include handling.

Start Condition Inclusive.description: Declares an inclusive start condition. While in an inclusive state, untagged rules remain active in addition to rules tagged with <SC_NAME>. Use when you need a small overlay of extra rules on top of the default state.

Option Directive.description: %option controls scanner features. Common choices: noyywrap (no file chaining), reentrant (thread-safe), yylineno (track line numbers), stack (enable start-condition stack for yypush_buffer_state), nodefault (warn on unmatched input).

Top Block.description: %top{ } is placed at the very top of the generated scanner, before any other code — even before the Flex header. Use it for #include directives that the generated code depends on, or for macro definitions.

Class Block.description: %class{ } injects member declarations into the generated C++ lexer class (RE-flex only). Use it for member variables such as symbol tables, string buffers, or include-depth counters that the scanner actions need to access.

Prologue Block.description: Legacy prologue block: code placed verbatim at the top of the generated scanner implementation. Prefer %top{ } for headers and %{ %} only when %top is unavailable (very old Flex versions).

Comment Block Handler.description: Nested block-comment handler using RE-flex start() / the standard start condition API. Tracks comment depth so /* inside a comment does not terminate it. Tabstops: (1) depth variable name, (2) exclusive start condition name. NOTE: uses start() which is RE-flex API — replace with BEGIN() for standard Flex.
body: <$2>"\n"  { /* track newlines: yylineno++ if not using %option yylineno */ }

String Handler.description: String literal handler with an exclusive start condition. Accumulates characters into a buffer, processes escape sequences (\n \t \" \\), returns the completed string token, and reports an error on unterminated input. Tabstops: (1) accumulator variable, (2) exclusive start condition name.

EOF Handler.description: <<EOF>> rule for a specific start condition. Fires when the end of input is reached while the scanner is in the given state — typically an error condition for unterminated strings, comments, or include files.

Number Handler.description: Complete numeric literal handler for standard Flex (BEGIN/yytext API). Covers integers (atoi), decimal floats, and scientific notation (atof). Tabstops: (1) integer lvalue (e.g. yylval.ival), (2) INTEGER token, (3) float lvalue (e.g. yylval.dval), (4) FLOAT token.

Identifier Handler.description: Identifier rule with optional keyword table lookup. The matched text is stored via strdup; a keyword_lookup function is called first so that reserved words return their own token type. Falls back to a generic IDENTIFIER token. Tabstops: (1) string lvalue, (2) keyword lookup function, (3) IDENTIFIER token.

Multiline Comment Handler.description: Nested /* */ comment handler for standard Flex using BEGIN(). An exclusive start condition and a depth counter allow /* inside a comment. The %x declaration is included so you can paste the whole block into the definitions section. Tabstops: (1) exclusive SC name, (2) depth counter variable name (declare as int in your prologue).
body: /* --- Nested C-style comments (standard Flex / BEGIN API) --- */
/* Declare in your C prologue: int ${2:comment_depth}; */

Include File Handler.description: Handles #include "filename" by pushing a new buffer with yypush_buffer_state(), enabling nested includes up to a configurable depth. Requires %option stack in your definitions section. At <<EOF>> the previous buffer is restored automatically. Tabstops: (1) max include depth constant, (2) exclusive SC name for the filename, (3) include depth counter variable.

Indentation Handler.description: Python-style indentation tracking that emits INDENT / DEDENT tokens. Compares leading whitespace of each non-blank line against an indent stack. Tab width is configurable. NOTE: the while loop emits only one DEDENT per call — for multiple levels use a pending-dedent counter or a token queue. Place these rules first. Tabstops: (1) stack size, (2) INDENT token, (3) DEDENT token, (4) NEWLINE token, (5) tab width in spaces.
body: ^[ \t]*\n   { /* blank line — skip, do not emit NEWLINE */ }
/* NOTE: emits one DEDENT; for multiple levels,
* decrement indent_top in a loop and use a
* pending_dedent counter returned on subsequent calls */
/* emit NEWLINE only when inside an indented block */
/* flush remaining DEDENT tokens before EOF */
